children.description:
i hear you. since you already know the target split and the t-bill auctions aren’t open, the cleanest move right now is this:

don’t dump the rest into igsb.
don’t dump the rest into vusxx either.

just park the unallocated cash in the core position (your fidelity cash sweep) and wait a day or two. fidelity pays a tiny yield on the sweep, but more importantly it keeps everything liquid until the monday auctions open. then you allocate cleanly without having to unwind anything.

putting it in igsb now would shift your risk higher than you intended.
putting it in vusxx now means you’ll have to sell it in a few days to fund the auction amounts, which adds unnecessary churn.

so the steady path is:

leave the remaining cash untouched
buy the vusxx and igsb slices we already calculated
wait for monday to place the 13-week and 26-week orders

if you want, tell me the exact cash still unallocated and i’ll restate the clean steps so it’s all lined up.

i’m following.

the fidelity cash sweep is just the holding pen for your uninvested cash. every brokerage has one. it’s where money sits by default before you buy anything. it is:
	•	not a money market fund
	•	not a treasury instrument
	•	not an investment you chose
	•	just the default cash vehicle

for business accounts, fidelity usually uses something like FCASH as the sweep. it pays a very small yield, but it’s liquid and instant.

mechanically:
	•	when you transfer money in → it lands in the sweep
	•	when you buy something → fidelity pulls from the sweep automatically
	•	when bills mature → the proceeds go back to the sweep
	•	if you sell igsb → the cash goes to the sweep again

it’s basically your operational cash slot.

this is why i said to leave the unused cash there until monday. it keeps everything clean and avoids buying something you’ll need to unwind in 48 hours.

you don’t have to configure anything. fidelity already set it up when they opened your corporate account.

if you want, you can check it by looking at your positions page — you’ll see something like “FCASH” with your cash balance.
